title: Battle of Tewkesbury 1471, Tewkesbury - 1000039
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Battle of Tewkesbury 1471, battlefield registration, Bloody Meadow/Gastons features, Severn Ham SSSI mention, and group value with Abbey Church of St Mary. Does not verify every Tewkesbury module subsite.

title: Abbey Church of St Mary, Tewkesbury - 1201159
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports the Abbey Church of St Mary / Tewkesbury Abbey as a listed heritage asset. Wider monastery chronology and all river-gate claims remain outside this source.

title: Hereford Cathedral - Home of the Mappa Mundi
notes: Directly supports Hereford Cathedral, Mappa Mundi, Chained Library, and preservation context. Does not verify all western marches places.

title: Dinedor Camp, Dinedor - 1001758
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Dinedor Camp as a scheduled promontory hillfort near the Wye/Lugg confluence. Does not verify all Herefordshire corridor sites.

title: Arthur's Stone, Dorstone, Herefordshire
notes: Directly supports Arthur's Stone as a Neolithic chambered tomb. The attached Arthur legend remains folklore and is not upgraded as factual history.

title: Woodchester Roman villa, Woodchester - 1004839
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Woodchester Roman Villa and the Orpheus Mosaic. Other Stroud/Frome radials remain unresolved by this source.

title: Uley Long Barrow (Hetty Pegler's Tump), Uley, Gloucestershire
notes: Directly supports Uley Long Barrow / Hetty Pegler's Tump as a Neolithic chambered long barrow. Does not verify every Stroud module site.

title: The Metalworking Evidence from West Hill, Uley, Gloucs.
notes: Directly supports West Hill, Uley as a Roman temple excavation context and metalworking evidence. Does not verify broader radial interpretation.

title: An Archaeological Survey in the Severn Vale, Gloucestershire: National Mapping Programme Highlight Report
notes: Supports Uley Bury as an Iron Age hillfort/settlement feature in the Severn Vale project. Other Stroud sites require separate records.

title: Corinium Museum - Discover the archaeology of the Cotswolds
notes: Supports Cirencester/Corinium, Roman town finds, Roman mosaics, and Corinium Dobunnorum as a fort/capital context. Does not verify all listed Corinium module subsites.

title: History of Cirencester Amphitheatre
notes: Directly supports Cirencester Amphitheatre, Corinium naming, early second-century construction, and Roman urban context. Other module places require further sources.

title: Chedworth Roman Villa, Gloucestershire
notes: Supports Chedworth Roman Villa as one of the largest second-century AD Roman villas in England with artefacts including mosaic fragments. Does not verify all Corinium module entries.

title: VCH Gloucestershire: Volume XVI, Bagendon
author_or_body: Victoria County History / Institute of Historical Research
notes: Supports Bagendon landscape, ancient routeways, brook/Churn context, and oolitic limestone geology. The oppidum claim still needs a direct archaeological source record.

title: Camp Hill promontory fort and Romano-British temple complex, Lydney - 1017373
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Lydney Park/Camp Hill, the Nodens temple complex, River Severn floodplain setting, Roman iron mining, and temple/baths/mansio structures. Does not verify Clearwell Caves, Puzzlewood, or all Forest of Dean entries.

title: Roman Villa at Clearwell Farm, Newland - 1406971
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Roman occupation near Clearwell and group value within a key Roman iron-working area. Does not directly verify Clearwell Caves or Puzzlewood coin hoard.

title: Research Framework for the Forest of Dean District
notes: Supports Forest of Dean archaeological research framing, Lydney/Nodens, Roman temple discussion, and Roman-period material context. Specific cave/hoard claims remain partly unresolved.

title: The Cotswold Way
notes: Directly supports the Cotswold Way as a 102-mile National Trail from Chipping Campden to Bath along the western edge of the Cotswold Hills. Does not verify Belas Knap or detailed Jurassic geology.

title: Belas Knap Long Barrow
notes: Directly supports Belas Knap as a Gloucestershire long barrow on the Cotswold Way and links it with other Gloucestershire Neolithic long barrows. Detailed geology still requires a geological source.

title: Roman Sacred Spring
author_or_body: The Roman Baths, Bath and North East Somerset Council
notes: Supports Bath Roman Baths, the Sacred Spring, Temple of Sulis Minerva, water feeding the Roman Baths, and the Roman drain to the River Avon. Does not verify all Bristol/Avon Gorge/Hwicce frontier elements.

title: The geology of the hot springs at Bath Spa
author_or_body: British Geological Survey / NERC Open Research Archive
notes: Supports Bath hot springs hydrogeology, Carboniferous Limestone, Mendip recharge model, spring pipes, artesian head, and geothermal heating. Hwicce political framing remains unsourced in Tier I.

title: Avon Gorge, Bristol, Avon
notes: Supports Avon Gorge geology, Avon Thrust, Hotwells Limestone, and fossiliferous limestone sections. Does not verify Bristol harbour or Hwicce frontier claims.

title: Severn Ham
notes: Supports Severn Ham as a 70-hectare SSSI on Severn Vale alluvium, winter flood area, committee-managed site, and access via bridges by Healings Mill and old Abbey Mill. Mill histories need further source records.

title: Healing's Mill
notes: Supports Healing’s Mill at the gateway to Tewkesbury where the Rivers Avon and Severn meet, with earliest parts dating from 1865 and listed building status. Abbey Mill and common-land records remain incomplete.

title: Staffordshire Hoard
notes: Supports Staffordshire Hoard as the largest hoard of Anglo-Saxon gold ever found, its display, conservation, and seventh-century Anglo-Saxon context.

title: Understanding the Staffordshire Hoard
notes: Supports Staffordshire Hoard research programme, Historic England funding, and museum stewardship. Does not verify other Mercian lattice sites.

title: History of Wroxeter Roman City
notes: Supports Wroxeter/Viroconium Roman city and its civic/urban history. Other module sites need separate records.

title: Old Oswestry hillfort, and two adjacent sections of Wat's Dyke - 1014899
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Old Oswestry hillfort as an earthwork and buried remains on a glacial mound north of Oswestry. Does not verify all central sandstone/cavity sites.

title: Geology of Wren's Nest National Nature Reserve
notes: Supports Wren's Nest geological reserve and Seven Sisters Mine limestone heritage. Wolverhampton Cross, Kinver, Lunt, Creswell, Nottingham caves and Borough Hill require further source records.

title: Lunt Roman Fort
author_or_body: Lunt Roman Fort / Coventry visitor heritage site
notes: Supports Lunt Roman Fort as a partially reconstructed timber fort with exhibition and gyrus feature. Detailed archaeological report still needed for stronger Tier I support.

title: Roman London's Temple of Mithras
notes: Supports London Mithraeum/Temple of Mithras, Walbrook/Londinium context, and Roman-period date. Does not verify Oxford/Ridgeway elements.

title: Port Meadow - Oxford's Outdoor Venue Guide
notes: Supports Port Meadow as SSSI, SAC, and scheduled-monument-consent landscape. Archaeological detail still needs direct records.

title: Dorchester-on-Thames
author_or_body: University of Oxford School of Archaeology
notes: Supports Dorchester-on-Thames as a key archaeological site with nationally important monuments and a walled Roman town area. London/Oxford corridor interpretation remains Tier II.

title: History of Wayland's Smithy
notes: Supports Wayland's Smithy as a two-phase Neolithic tomb built along the Ridgeway close to the Vale of White Horse. Folklore remains non-Tier-I.

title: History at White Horse Hill
notes: Supports White Horse Hill, Bronze Age white horse, Iron Age hillfort, and Wayland Smithy landscape. Dragon/St George legends remain folklore and are not factual evidence.

title: Earthworks of Car Dyke in Park Wood - 1009999
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Car Dyke as a Roman canal/earthwork on the Fen edge, low-lying Fenland Roman canals, waterlogged deposits, and Jurassic clays. Does not verify all Fenland module elements.

title: Must Farm Project
author_or_body: University of Cambridge Department of Archaeology / Cambridge Archaeological Unit
notes: Supports deep Fenland landscape-scale archaeology and a waterlogged site excavated with Cambridge Archaeological Unit. This supports the waterlogged timber settlement theme generally but not every Fen-edge villa or drainage claim.

title: Fleam Dyke
author_or_body: Wildlife Trust for Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire and Northamptonshire
notes: Supports Fleam Dyke as a major Cambridgeshire earthwork, Scheduled Ancient Monument and SSSI. Historic England direct record still preferred for strongest source support.

title: Bran Ditch and Cambridgeshire Dykes context - 1410907
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Fleam and Devil's Dykes as part of the protected Cambridgeshire Dykes group and gives early medieval/post-Roman context. Does not directly source Bedford Level drainage.

title: Avebury's stone circles and henge
notes: Supports Avebury henge/stone circles, West Kennet Avenue, Silbury Hill, West Kennet Long Barrow, and broader Neolithic/Bronze Age monument landscape. Swindon hillfort/Roman elements need separate records.

title: The Avebury World Heritage Site
notes: Supports Avebury World Heritage landscape, Silbury Hill, West Kennet Long Barrow, West Kennet Avenue and the Sanctuary. Does not verify Barbury/Liddington/Wanborough/Coate Water.

title: West Kennet long barrow, 800m south-east of Silbury Hill - 1010628
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports West Kennet Long Barrow and the Avebury/Silbury monument context.

title: Great Witcombe Romano-British villa - 1014826
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Great Witcombe Roman Villa, its position beneath the Cotswold scarp, springs above the villa, nearby stream, and villa group around Roman Gloucester. Does not verify all Gloucester radial locations.

title: Horsbere Brook Flood Alleviation Scheme
notes: Supports Horsbere Brook flood storage and Gloucester hydrology/flood-management context. Does not verify NATO/military overlays, Framilode, Hardwicke, Hawkesbury, Brockworth, Cooper’s Hill, or Witcombe reservoirs.

title: History of Odda's Chapel
notes: Directly supports Odda's Chapel at Deerhurst as a surviving Saxon chapel, its relation to Deerhurst Priory/St Mary's, the Odda Stone, Edward the Confessor/Westminster Abbey reference, local blue lias stone, and Deerhurst Saxon/priory context.

title: Kinver Edge and the Rock Houses
notes: Supports Kinver Edge and Holy Austin Rock Houses as homes carved into sandstone on a sandstone ridge with an Iron Age hillfort landscape. Does not verify wider Mercian lattice interpretation.

title: Twenty years since the rock art discovery at Creswell Crags
notes: Supports Creswell Crags as a Palaeolithic cave-art site. Use for the named item only; detailed dating and wider cave archaeology need item-specific academic sources.

title: City of Caves
notes: Supports Nottingham City of Caves as a major sandstone cave network beneath Nottingham. Does not verify all historical uses at item level.

title: Borough Hill: two Iron Age hillforts and a defended enclosure, two Bronze Age barrows, a Roman building complex and barrow cemetery - 1010696
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Borough Hill as a scheduled monument with Iron Age hillforts, Bronze Age barrows, Roman building complex, and barrow cemetery.

title: Barbury Castle: a hillfort and bowl barrow - 1014557
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Barbury Castle as a scheduled hillfort and bowl barrow. Wider Ridgeway gateway interpretation remains Tier II.

title: Liddington Castle - 1016312
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Liddington Castle as a scheduled slight univallate hillfort.

title: Site of Roman town, W of Wanborough House - 1004684
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports a scheduled Roman town west of Wanborough House. The record is a legacy OCN entry and does not itself verify the Durocornovium place-name; claim therefore remains partial.

title: The Ridgeway - National Trails
notes: Supports the Ridgeway as an official National Trail through southern central England. Ancient-route interpretation should be separately sourced before being treated as Tier I.

title: Cooper's Hill Nature Reserve
notes: Supports Cooper's Hill as a Brockworth local nature reserve with SSSI woodland/limestone grassland context and an Iron Age settlement reference. Needs stronger primary HER or Historic England support for archaeological detail.

title: Fretherne and Saul: Introduction / Economic History
author_or_body: Victoria County History via British History Online
notes: Used as a VCH/BHO anchor for Framilode/Fretherne/Saul context. Exact subpage URL needs item-level confirmation; claim remains partial.

title: Gloucestershire welcomes the ARRC with formal cathedral service
notes: Supports public NATO/ARRC overlay at Imjin Barracks, Innsworth, through a GOV.UK source. Does not support any classified or non-public military detail.

title: The Church of St Mary, Deerhurst - 1151998
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports St Mary's Deerhurst as a Grade I listed former monastery / parish church. Detailed Saxon chronology should use the full list entry or specialist architectural sources.

title: Deerhurst monastic site and multi-period settlement - 1018632
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Supports Deerhurst monastic site, multi-period settlement, St Mary's, Priory Farmhouse, Odda's Chapel group value, and Edmund Ironside/Cnut context.

title: Mercia Mudstone Group
notes: Supports Mercia Mudstone Group as a formal geological unit. Does not by itself prove exact Deerhurst/Apperley site geology without map-sheet or borehole confirmation.

title: Metchley Roman Forts. A Programme of Archaeological Investigations
notes: Supports Metchley Roman forts and archaeological investigations around the fort and annexes. Use only for the Roman-fort item, not wider lattice interpretation.

title: Anglian cross 25m south of St Peter's Collegiate Church - 1005886
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports the Wolverhampton Anglian cross location and scheduled-monument status. Roman-column reuse is not asserted in the claim.

title: Parks and open spaces in Swindon - Coate Water
notes: Supports Coate Water Country Park, reservoir origin in the 1820s as a Wilts and Berks Canal headwater tank, Local Nature Reserve status, and SSSI wildlife context.

title: Stone circle immediately north east of Day House, Coate - 1016359
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports the scheduled stone circle immediately north east of Day House, Coate. Does not verify broader Coate Water stone-alignment speculation.

title: Coopers Hill
notes: Supports Cooper's Hill as part of High Brotheridge Camp Scheduled Ancient Monument and a possible Iron Age hillfort. Primary NHLE/HER record still needed for full archaeological verification.

title: Hawkesbury - Domesday Book
author_or_body: Open Domesday / Professor J.J.N. Palmer and team
notes: Supports Hawkesbury in Grimboldestou hundred with 52 households in 1086, Pershore Abbey ownership, resources, value, and Phillimore reference Gloucestershire 14,2.

title: Deerhurst - Domesday Book
author_or_body: Open Domesday / Professor J.J.N. Palmer and team
notes: Supports Deerhurst Domesday entry with 34 households, Westminster Abbey ownership in 1066 and 1086, land/resources, value, and Phillimore reference Gloucestershire 19,1.

title: Hundred of Deerhurst - Domesday Book
author_or_body: Open Domesday / Professor J.J.N. Palmer and team
notes: Supports Hardwicke as a place listed in the Hundred of Deerhurst with 10.9 households. Exact manor-level entry details require a directly retrievable Hardwicke place page or folio transcription.

title: Brockworth Court - 1091764
author_or_body: Historic England / National Heritage List for England
notes: Directly supports Brockworth Court as a Grade II* listed building in Brockworth, built for Richard Hart, last prior of Lanthony, in 1534-9.

title: Parish History
notes: Supports Brockworth parish history, including St George's Church, Brockworth Court, Brockworth Mill, and the statement that Witcombe Reservoir was built in 1863 and affected the mill. Treat as parish-history support rather than primary archaeology.

title: Tewkesbury, sheet 216, memoir for 1:50 000 geological map
notes: Supports regional Tewkesbury-sheet Mercia Mudstone and alluvium descriptions, including stiff grey to brownish grey clay floodplain alluvium. Use as local geological context, not exact site-borehole proof.

title: A417 Missing Link: National Trust Written Representation
author_or_body: National Trust / Planning Inspectorate document library
notes: Supports Witcombe Reservoir as a late 19th-century feature in heritage/planning context. Further water-company or local authority reservoir records needed for full hydrology history.

title: Apperley and Deerhurst History
notes: Supports Apperley as part of the Deerhurst/Apperley local-history context. Use as partial support only until VCH, parish, or HER records are attached.
